summary: The PR adds markdown support, but it also regresses both automation-facing JSON entry points: the no-flag CLI default now emits markdown, and explicit json routing now returns the markdown renderer.

findings.title: Changing the CLI default to markdown breaks no-flag automation callers
findings.impact: The unchanged export contract says nightly automation shells out without flags and expects machine-readable JSON with a top-level version field. Flipping the parser default to markdown means the same no-flag invocation now prints human-readable markdown instead, so existing automation consumers stop receiving the JSON payload the PR description promised to preserve.

findings.title: The json renderer registry entry now routes explicit json requests to markdown
findings.impact: The unchanged CLI json branch still assumes export_report(..., output='json') returns a dictionary-like payload and serializes it with json.dumps. After this registry change, explicit --output json resolves to render_markdown, so callers get a quoted markdown string instead of the historical object shape, violating the documented byte-for-byte json compatibility guarantee for downstream consumers.
